meaning: adj.易曲折的,柔软的,圆滑的,柔韧的
examples: Birch bark is pliable and very easy to bend.
The four states make up the brass buckle on that tough but pliable leather belt.
As your baby grows bigger, his bones become less pliable.
But through British patronage it grew, creating for the colonialists a pliable tribal elite.
But the fact remains that Saddam Hussein could not have asked for a more pliable character than Hans Blix.
